vocabulary.name: OpenSky Network API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Domain vocabulary for the OpenSky Network REST API covering aviation, ADS-B flight tracking, and aircraft state concepts.

terms.definition: Automatic Dependent Surveillance-Broadcast. A surveillance technology in which an aircraft determines its position via satellite navigation and periodically broadcasts it enabling it to be tracked.
A unique 24-bit ICAO (International Civil Aviation Organization) address assigned to each aircraft transponder. Represented as a 6-character lower-case hex string.
A data record representing the instantaneous state of an aircraft at a given point in time, including position, altitude, velocity, and identification data.
The radio call sign of a flight, typically 8 characters or fewer. May differ from the flight number shown to passengers.
The ICAO 24-bit transponder address of an aircraft, represented as a 6-character lower-case hex string (e.g., 3c6444).
The country of registration of an aircraft, inferred from its ICAO 24-bit address block assignment.
Barometric altitude in meters, derived from the aircraft's transponder pressure altitude reading.
Geometric altitude in meters, typically GPS-derived altitude above the WGS-84 ellipsoid.
A boolean flag indicating whether the aircraft is on the ground, derived from the surface movement report in the ADS-B message.
Ground speed of the aircraft in meters per second.
The direction of the aircraft's movement over the ground measured in decimal degrees clockwise from true north.
The rate of change of altitude in meters per second. Positive values indicate climbing; negative values indicate descending.
A 4-digit octal code broadcast by the aircraft transponder used by air traffic control for identification. Also known as the transponder code.
Special Purpose Indicator. A flag in the ADS-B message indicating a special flight status, such as emergency or military.
The source technology used to derive the aircraft's position. Values: 0=ADS-B, 1=ASTERIX, 2=MLAT, 3=FLARM.
Multilateration. A technique for determining aircraft position by measuring the time difference of arrival of signals at multiple ground receivers.
All Purpose STructured Eurocontrol suRveillance Information eXchange. A binary format used for transmitting surveillance data in European air traffic management.
Flight Alarm. A traffic awareness and collision avoidance technology widely used by general aviation and gliders in Europe.
Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch) of the last position update received for an aircraft. Null if no position was received in the last 15 seconds.
Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch) of the last message received from the aircraft transponder for any message type.
Unix timestamp of the estimated time of departure for a flight record.
Unix timestamp of the estimated time of arrival for a flight record.
The ICAO airport code of the estimated departure airport for a flight. Null if the departure airport could not be identified.
The ICAO airport code of the estimated arrival airport for a flight. Null if the arrival airport could not be identified.
World Geodetic System 1984. The global standard coordinate reference system used for GPS and most aviation navigation systems.
The unit of rate limit consumption used by the OpenSky Network API. Each request consumes a number of credits depending on the endpoint and query scope.
A time-ordered sequence of waypoints representing the trajectory of an aircraft over a flight.
A single point in an aircraft track containing time, latitude, longitude, altitude, heading, and ground status.
